description_long: This object is a small, cylindrical lotion container with a white body and green printed text and graphics. The container appears to be made of plastic and has a slightly crumpled or used appearance, with visible creases and dents along its surface. The top features a white, twisted dispenser nozzle, likely for squeezing out the lotion, and is capped with a matching white closure. The label includes the brand name 'Raggio di Sole' and other Italian text, with some areas of the print faded or worn. The overall finish is matte with a slightly rough texture, and the graphics are simple, consisting mainly of text and rectangular blocks. The object is compact and lightweight, suitable for personal use and easy to carry or dispose of in an urban environment.
description: A small, white and green cylindrical lotion container with a twisted dispenser nozzle and printed Italian text, showing signs of use and a matte, slightly rough finish.
description_view_0: The top and side of the container are visible, highlighting the twisted dispenser nozzle and the crumpled, white plastic body.
description_view_1: The front label is most visible, showing the 'Raggio di Sole' brand and other printed text, with the dispenser nozzle seen from above.
description_view_2: The side view emphasizes the curvature of the container and the partially visible label, with the nozzle pointing upward.
description_view_3: The back and top of the container are shown, with measurement markings and less visible branding, focusing on the cap and nozzle.

license_info.creator_display_name: Archeoplastica: il museo dei rifiuti spiaggiati
